description:
You are a senior fullstack engineer.

Project stack:

BACKEND:
- NestJS 10
- MongoDB with Mongoose
- TypeScript
- DTO + class-validator
- Clean REST architecture
- Standard exception handling

FRONTEND:
- Next.js 16 (App Router)
- React 19
- TypeScript
- shadcn/ui
- @tanstack/react-query v5
- axios
- react-hook-form
- zod
- sonner (toast)

==================================================
TASK: CREATE FULL PAYMENT MANAGEMENT (BACKEND + FRONTEND)
==================================================

Context:
Payment belongs to an Event.
One Event can have many Payments.

==================================================
PART 1 — BACKEND (NestJS)
==================================================

ENTITY: PAYMENT

FIELDS:
- event: ObjectId (required, ref Event)
- userName: string (required)
- userEmail: string (required)
- amount: number (required, >= 0)
- paymentMethod: string (required, enum: "CASH" | "BANK_TRANSFER" | "MOMO")
- status: string (required, enum: "PENDING" | "SUCCESS" | "FAILED")
- transactionId: string (optional)
- paidAt: Date (optional)
- timestamps enabled

==================================================
FOLDER STRUCTURE
==================================================

src/payment/
  payment.module.ts
  payment.controller.ts
  payment.service.ts
  dto/
    create-payment.dto.ts
    update-payment.dto.ts
    query-payment.dto.ts
  schemas/
    payment.schema.ts

==================================================
ENDPOINTS
==================================================

GET    /payments
GET    /payments/:id
POST   /payments
PUT    /payments/:id
DELETE /payments/:id

PATCH  /payments/:id/status   (update status only)

==================================================
GET /payments REQUIREMENTS
==================================================

Query params:
- page: number | "all"
- limit: number
- search: string (search by userName or userEmail)
- eventId: string (filter by event)
- status: string (filter by status)

Pagination rules:
- Default page = 1
- Default limit = 10
- If page = "all" → return all
- Sort by createdAt DESC

Search rule:

{
  $or: [
    { userName: { $regex: search, $options: "i" } },
    { userEmail: { $regex: search, $options: "i" } }
  ]
}

Must populate:
- event (select title, price)

==================================================
RETURN LIST FORMAT
==================================================

{
  data: Payment[],
  pagination: {
    total: number,
    page: number,
    limit: number,
    totalPages: number,
    hasNextPage: boolean,
    hasPrevPage: boolean
  }
}

==================================================
VALIDATION
==================================================

CreatePaymentDto:
- event required
- userName required
- userEmail required and must be email
- amount required and >= 0
- paymentMethod required (enum)
- status optional (default PENDING)
- transactionId optional

UpdatePaymentDto:
- all optional

QueryPaymentDto:
- page optional
- limit optional
- search optional
- eventId optional
- status optional

==================================================
BUSINESS RULES
==================================================

- When status = SUCCESS → set paidAt = current date
- When status != SUCCESS → paidAt = null
- Throw NotFoundException if payment not found
- Throw BadRequestException for invalid enum or invalid ObjectId
- Must validate Event exists when creating Payment

==================================================
CODE REQUIREMENTS
==================================================

- Fully typed
- Production-ready
- No pseudo code
- No missing files
- Clean architecture
- Proper population typing
- Proper enum implementation

Generate complete code for every file.
Do not skip any file.

==================================================
PART 2 — FRONTEND (Next.js)
==================================================

Route:

app/dashboard/payments/page.tsx

Folder structure:

app/dashboard/payments/
  page.tsx
  components/
    PaymentTable.tsx
    PaymentForm.tsx
    PaymentDialog.tsx

lib/api/payment.api.ts
types/payment.type.ts

==================================================
FEATURES
==================================================

- Payment list table
- Search (300ms debounce)
- Filter by:
    - Event (Select dropdown)
    - Status (Select dropdown)
- Pagination
- Create payment
- Edit payment
- Delete payment (confirm dialog)
- Update status directly from table (Select)
- Show event title
- Show formatted amount (currency format)
- Show payment method badge
- Show status badge (color coded)
- Show paidAt date
- Loading state
- Empty state
- Toast notifications

==================================================
FORM VALIDATION (ZOD)
==================================================

const paymentSchema = z.object({
  event: z.string().min(1, "Event is required"),
  userName: z.string().min(1, "User name is required"),
  userEmail: z.string().email("Invalid email"),
  amount: z.number().min(0, "Amount must be >= 0"),
  paymentMethod: z.enum(["CASH", "BANK_TRANSFER", "MOMO"]),
  status: z.enum(["PENDING", "SUCCESS", "FAILED"]).optional(),
  transactionId: z.string().optional()
})

==================================================
IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS
==================================================

Frontend:

- React Query v5:
  - useQuery for list
  - useMutation for create/update/delete
  - useMutation for update status
  - invalidate properly

- Axios base instance
- shadcn components:
  - Table
  - Input
  - Button
  - Dialog
  - AlertDialog
  - Select
  - Badge
  - Pagination

- Format currency using Intl.NumberFormat
- Show colored badges:
    SUCCESS → green
    FAILED → red
    PENDING → yellow

==================================================
DASHBOARD MENU
==================================================

Add new menu item in dashboard sidebar:

- Payments → /dashboard/payments
- Use lucide-react icon: CreditCard

==================================================
IMPORTANT
==================================================

- Generate full working backend and frontend code
- Do not skip any file
- No pseudo code
- Clean architecture
- Fully typed
- Production-ready
